name: What is the best flooring for pets and sand near the beach?

mainEntity.name: What flooring is best for homes with pets and beach sand in Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?
mainEntity.text: What flooring is best for homes with pets and beach sand in Pacifica or Half Moon Bay?

acceptedAnswer.text: Homes near the beach need flooring that can handle sand, wet paws, and frequent cleanup. Pet claws and tracked-in grit can wear down softer surfaces quickly, so scratch resistance matters. Luxury vinyl plank is popular because it is easy to clean, water resistant, and generally more forgiving than wood. Tile is even harder wearing, though it can be less comfortable underfoot. Engineered wood offers a warmer, more traditional look, but it usually needs more care and may show scratches sooner. If you have large dogs, multiple pets, or frequent beach traffic, a tougher surface is usually the better investment than a delicate one. Also consider texture and finish. A matte or lightly textured floor often hides sand and minor scratches better than a shiny one. Ask the installer about recommended transitions, base trim, and underlayment so the final result stands up to daily use in a coastal home.
acceptedAnswer.description: Luxury vinyl plank and tile are usually the best choices for pets and beach sand because they resist scratches, moisture, and easy cleanup. Engineered wood can work too, but it is more vulnerable to scratching and water than vinyl or tile.
